description: Create the final visual-debugging and E2E validation pipeline: PTY artifacts, ANSI visual artifacts or fallback, golden/semantic screens, Web checks, installed CLI smoke, and completion evidence report.

userStories.title: Standardize PTY rendering-parity artifact capture
userStories.description: As a Ralph agent, I want repeatable PTY scenarios that leave enough evidence to review what the terminal rendered.
acceptanceCriteria: Add or extend a rendering-parity PTY script/scenario runner with bounded timeout, idle timeout, rows/cols, NO_COLOR support, deterministic env support, expect/reject assertions, and clear failure diagnostics
Every scenario writes raw.ansi.log, clean.txt, screen.txt, events.jsonl when available, metadata.json, input.json or equivalent, and assertions.json or equivalent

Scenarios cover startup owner/room/session picker, slash palette filter/run, /status without picker, /status while picker is open, mixed transcript, /thinking, /model or /login deterministic nested flow, narrow width, and NO_COLOR=1
Scenario docs include host and Docker-worker command examples and warn that artifacts may contain transcripts or secrets
Focused PTY runner tests pass
When marking passes true, notes include commands run, evidence tier, artifact paths where applicable, and fake/demo/mocked/real classification
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Generate reviewable ANSI visual artifacts or documented fallback
userStories.description: As a reviewer, I want screenshot-like terminal artifacts without rerunning the TUI.
acceptanceCriteria: Investigate and wire an ANSI-to-HTML, ANSI-to-SVG, ANSI-to-PNG, or terminal-emulator screenshot path for at least one rendering-parity PTY scenario
Visual artifact generation command is documented and reproducible from the artifact directory or raw ANSI file
Report links at least one visual artifact showing status card, slash palette, picker, or mixed transcript
If conversion is unavailable, committed report documents the reason, exact attempted commands, raw/clean/screen fallback paths, and follow-up task
Visual artifact or fallback never requires production gateway restart or live credentials
Focused visual-artifact tests or script dry-run checks pass where feasible
When marking passes true, notes include commands run, evidence tier, artifact paths where applicable, and fake/demo/mocked/real classification
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add golden and semantic screen regression checks
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want automated checks that fail when the TUI drifts back to a dashboard or detached-message layout.
acceptanceCriteria: Golden or normalized screen tests cover slash palette layout, compact status card with bars, room/session picker layout, mixed transcript row order/markers, narrow output, and NO_COLOR output

Semantic tests assert row kind/status order, user vs assistant marker difference, command result placement above prompt, status bar availability/unavailability, secret redaction, and streaming order preservation
Dynamic values such as ids, timestamps, cwd, token counts, and ANSI color codes are normalized so tests are stable but still meaningful
Tests reject top-level status payloads, dashboard-style status dumps, dominant long UUID labels, and command results rendered only in state.message where detectable
Focused golden/semantic tests pass
When marking passes true, notes include commands run, evidence tier, artifact paths where applicable, and fake/demo/mocked/real classification
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Complete Web regression and shared-fixture validation
userStories.description: As a Web user, I want Web Compact Terminal to remain the semantic reference while Ink reaches parity.
acceptanceCriteria: Web Compact Terminal source or renderer tests prove shared row/card/status descriptors are still used for status, model, login, thinking, tool, command, and error fixtures where supported
Stable Web semantic hooks cover shared terminal card kind, status fields, row kind/status, event/run/order metadata, and redacted content where feasible
Where browser tooling is available, capture or document a Web Compact Terminal screenshot/reference for the same shared fixture or explain why source/render tests are the current reference
npm run chat-ui:typecheck passes
npm run chat-ui:build passes
Focused Web regression tests pass
When marking passes true, notes include commands run, evidence tier, artifact paths where applicable, and fake/demo/mocked/real classification
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Run final installed CLI and full validation gates
userStories.description: As a release reviewer, I want final default-path proof before calling terminal rendering parity complete.
acceptanceCriteria: npm run typecheck passes
npm test passes or any pre-existing unrelated failure is explicitly proven with focused passing checks and documented risk before completion is withheld/escalated
npm run chat-ui:typecheck passes
npm run chat-ui:build passes

Build and install the package globally in the dedicated worker or test environment, then run installed pibo tui:sessions through owner/room/session/message, slash palette, /status, picker-open /status, and narrow/NO_COLOR smoke where feasible
Installed/global PTY artifacts include raw ANSI, clean text, final screen, metadata, assertions, exact commands, artifact paths, and fake/demo/mocked/real classification
At least one default-path validation uses installed/global pibo tui:sessions, not only npm run dev or renderToString
When marking passes true, notes include commands run, evidence tier, artifact paths where applicable, and fake/demo/mocked/real classification
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Publish final parity evidence report
userStories.description: As a human reviewer, I want one report that states exactly what matches Web Compact Terminal behavior and what remains out of scope.
acceptanceCriteria: Add final report under docs/reports/ summarizing implemented behavior, changed files, validation commands, PTY artifact directories, visual artifact/fallback paths, Web checks, and installed CLI evidence
Report maps final evidence to SC-001 through SC-006 from spec.md and to PRDs 01-07
Report names remaining gaps, explicitly out-of-scope items, environment limitations, and any live-provider/provider-catalog limitations
Report includes a real/default-path evidence audit for user-facing paths and states whether any path relies only on fake/demo/mocked support
README PRD catalog updates Ralph JSON links and final validation instructions
Focused docs/report checks pass where available
When marking passes true, notes include commands run, evidence tier, artifact paths where applicable, and fake/demo/mocked/real classification
Typecheck passes
